title: Add /login and /register API route endpoints
description: Create Next.js route handlers at src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts and src/app/api/auth/register/route.ts to expose the existing login/register business logic as POST HTTP endpoints. The existing src/api/auth/login.ts and src/api/auth/register.ts provide the business logic; this task creates the API surface layer.
existing_patterns: src/app/api/health/route.ts - minimal route handler pattern with JSON response
src/app/api/courses/search/route.ts - route handler using withAuth, getPayloadInstance, and JSON responses
src/api/auth/register.ts - existing register business logic using AuthService and Payload
src/api/auth/login.ts - existing login business logic using UserStore and SessionStore
src/auth/withAuth.ts - auth decorator pattern for route handlers
questions: The task AC says /register accepts { email, password } but existing register.ts requires confirmPassword. Should confirmPassword be removed to match the AC?
Existing code uses AuthService (PBKDF2) for register but UserStore (SHA-256) for login — inconsistent. Should login be updated to use AuthService for consistency?
The task AC says duplicate email returns 400 but existing register.ts returns 409. Which status code should be used?
